standard: can understand simple classroom instructions.
example: Clap your hands _ times / Set up / Zip your mouth zip zip / Hands on the table / Eyes on me
question: Stand up. Now clap your hands. And Sit down. Put your hands together.

standard: can listen and point to familiar objects.
example: T says cat -> S points to the cat picture.
question: Point to the cat. / Where's the dog? / Show me the ball.

standard: can recognize familiar words when I hear them.
example: Joins in Head, Shoulders, Knees and Toes / 2 fingers crossing turn into rabbit...
question: Follow me: Head, Shoulders, Knees and Toes / 2 fingers crossing(x2) turn into a rabbit...

standard: can respond with actions, gestures, or single words.
example: Waves goodbye. Nods for yes. Shakes head for no.
question: Greeting with shaking hands and Say Goodbye with waving arm

standard: can identify individual letter sounds when heard clearly.
example: What starts with /b/? -> S points to ball, bus, book.
question: Teacher touch face and say /b/ /b/ ball and student can repeat with the right mouth form.

standard: can repeat single words after the teacher.
example: T says Apple. -> S says Apple.
question: Can you repeat after me? /c/ /c/ cat 1 2 3 ...

standard: can produce basic greetings.
example: Hi! / Hello! / Bye bye! / Good morning, afternoon / See you soon.
question: Greeting by saying Hello! and Say Goodbye by saying Bye Bye!

standard: can use gestures and actions to support communication.
example: Waves goodbye. Nods for yes. Shakes head for no.
question: Introduce simple 'Simon says'

standard: can understand common classroom language.
example: Open your book. / Look at the board. / Listen carefully. Excuse me? Raise hand before speak.
question: Open your book. Close your book. Listen to Teacher. Look at this one.

standard: can recognise and respond to basic action words.
example: Reacts correctly to jump / run / sit / stop.
question: Introduce simple 'Simon says' with action words

standard: can understand a simple self-introduction.
example: My name is Cathy. I'm your Teacher.
question: My name is Teacher Cathy. What's my name again? Cathy or Thyca.

standard: can state their own name when asked.
example: My name is Natee. or simply Natee! when prompted.
question: What's your name? How old are you?

standard: can respond with Yes or No.
example: Do you like cats? -> Yes! / No!
question: Do you like dog? Do you like cat?

standard: can count from 1 to 10.
example: Counts objects aloud: One, two, three... ten!
question: How many apples? Can you count the apples? What number is this?

standard: can name basic colors, animals, and classroom objects.
example: Red / Dog / Pencil / Chair
question: What color is this? / What's this animal?

standard: can answer personal questions.
example: This is my mum. She has long hair.
question: Show me the girl with long hair.

standard: can understand basic What & Who questions.
example: What is this? / Who is she? -> S points after hearing questions.
question: Show the picture of things or choose sth. around Student: What is this? / Who is she?

standard: can follow a short illustrated dialogue while listening.
example: Follows a picture-book dialogue as teacher reads aloud.
question: Who is speaking now? Peter Rabbit or Mr.Gregor

standard: can give a basic self-introduction.
example: My name is Jadi. I'm six. I'm Thai / I live in Thailand.
question: What's your name? How old are you? Where do you live?

standard: can answer simple questions with 1-2 words.
example: What color? -> Blue! / How many? -> Three!
question: Show restaurant photo: What's this? Expected Answer: Pizza or Ice cream.

standard: can talk about my likes and dislikes.
example: I like cats. / I don't like spiders.
question: Tell me one thing you like / don't like. What animal do you like?

standard: can reproduce a simple song from memory.
example: Sings 'I can jump, I can walk' or classroom chants.
question: Can you sing any English song? Baby shark...

standard: can understand sentences describing physical appearance with picture support.
example: Guess who: She has curly hair and blue eyes. -> S points to correct picture.
question: Which one is the big red apple?

standard: can understand ability statements (can/can't) from slow, clear speech.
example: He can swim but he can't ride a bike.
question: Look at the animals. Point to the correct one based on abilities.

standard: can understand positional language in simple sentences.
example: The cat is under the table. -> S points to correct picture.
question: Find the ball: The ball is under the chair

standard: can understand a short message about time and place.
example: Meet me at the park at 3 o'clock.
question: Meet me at the zoo at 10 o'clock. Point to the right place in time.

standard: can understand short social exchanges when spoken slowly.
example: How are you? / I'm good I'm great I'm wonderful, And you?
question: How are you today?

standard: can state their birthday (day and month).
example: My birthday is on the 5th of May.
question: When is your birthday?

standard: can name parts of the body.
example: head / shoulders / knees / toes / eyes / ears.
question: Head, Shoulders, Knees and Toes game. What is this body part?

standard: can ask and answer by themself.
example: What's this? / What color is it? -> It's a book. / It's green.
question: Mystery Object: Student asks What color is it?

standard: can participate in classroom games using fixed expressions.
example: I'm ready / Our turn! / My turn! / Your turn! / Their turn / I win!
question: Play Tic Tac Toe (X/O) using English game expressions

standard: can understand short social exchanges.
example: How are you? What's the weather like today?
question: How are you today?

standard: can understand a description of a daily routine.
example: I wake up at seven. I eat breakfast and go to school.
question: What time do you wake up?

standard: can follow a simple dialogue about everyday activities.
example: 2 children talking about what they do after school.
question: Read: Zeno always plays with Oscar at the playground after school. They love to run together. Ask: Who always plays with Zeno?

standard: can produce complete sentences independently.
example: I go to school by car. / My dog is small and brown.
question: How do you go to school? What's this color? (Answer in full sentences)

standard: can describe their own daily routine.
example: Present simple tense usage.
question: Tell me about your morning. What do you do when you wake up?

standard: can ask and answer questions about everyday life.
example: Do you have a pet? -> Yes, I have a cat.
question: Do you have a pet? Do you like carrots?

standard: can extract key information from a short audio without pictures.
example: Listens and answers Where did she go? / What did he buy?
question: Where did Mother say before she leaves? / What did Peter do? / What did he eat from the garden?

standard: can follow basic step-by-step instructions.
example: First, draw a circle. Then, draw two eyes. Finally, color it blue.
question: Follow instruction: First, cut the paper. Then, fold it. Now, draw a face.

standard: can follow a short story with characters and a simple plot.
example: First, cut the paper. Then, fold it. Now, draw a face.
question: What happened first? What happened next? How did the story end?

standard: can perform a short role play using a script.
example: How much is this? / What's the matter?
question: Teacher asks 'Can I help you?' as a seller.

standard: can talk about a past event using simple past tense.
example: Yesterday I played football with my friends.
question: What did you do last weekend?

standard: can explain the picture.
example: Describes people, actions, and objects in a picture.
question: Tell me about this picture.

standard: can understand the main points of a two-person dialogue.
example: 2 people plan a trip -> Student answers Where are they going?
question: Where are they going? Why are they going there? What are they planning to do?

standard: can identify a speaker's feelings from tone of voice.
example: She sounds excited, sad, surprised. -> picks the right face.
question: How does the speaker feel?

standard: can extract key details (who, what, where) from a longer audio.
example: After listening to a 1-minute clip. Student notes 3 facts.
question: Who is the story about? What happened? Where did it happen?

standard: can open, sustain, and close a simple conversation.
example: Hey! Did you watch the game? ... OK, see you later!
question: Talk with the teacher about a hobby and finish politely.

standard: can exchange information on a familiar topic for 3-4 turns.
example: Talks about favourite food with a partner without stopping.
question: What is your favourite food? Why do you like it? When do you eat it?

standard: can deviate slightly from a script during role play.
example: Adds an unscripted line when it feels natural.
question: Buy a ticket. Teacher asks an unexpected question: 'Who are you travelling with?' Respond naturally.

standard: can understand the main points of a conversation about everyday topics.
example: Connect ideas of 2 students discussing their weekend -> S summaries what happened.
question: Where did they go? What was the most interesting activity?

standard: can follow a group conversation with 3+ speakers.
example: Class debate -> Who said they liked cats? Who disagreed?
question: Who likes cats? Who prefers dogs?

standard: can understand conversations without visual support.
example: Listen to two friends talking on the phone and answer questions.
question: What are they going to do?

standard: can understand multi-step verbal instructions.
example: Follows a complex game rule explained without demonstration.
question: Draw a square. Write your name inside it. Draw a star above it. Circle the star.

standard: can ask for more information.
example: Really? Why? What happened next?
question: I can do a magic trick. Expected student response: 'Really? How?'

standard: can give a reason for a preference.
example: I like summer because I can swim.
question: What's your favourite season? Why do you like it?

standard: can maintain conversations independently.
example: I always play Roblox with my best friend.
question: What is your favourite hobby? How often do you do it?

standard: can express agreement or disagreement politely.
example: I think so too. / Hmm, I'm not sure about that.
question: Do you think children should have homework every day?

standard: can discuss familiar topics in detail.
example: He thinks homework is too much because he has football training.
question: Why does he think homework is too much? Do you agree with him?

standard: can follow an extended conversation at natural speed.
example: Follows a 3-minute kids' podcast without needing it repeated.
question: What was the podcast mainly about?

standard: can note specific details from a longer listening text.
example: Listens to a short story about a school trip.
question: Where did they go? / Who went on the trip? / What did they do there?

standard: can ask for clarification when I do not understand.
example: Sorry, could you say that again?
question: What do you say if you don't understand me?

standard: can give suggestions.
example: You should join the football club.
question: What should I do if I'm bored after school?

standard: can explain problems and solutions.
example: My backpack is too heavy, so I leave some books at school.
question: Tell me about a problem and how to fix it.

standard: can participate actively in group discussions.
example: Shares ideas and responds to others.
question: Do you agree or disagree with this? Why?
